hero_subtitle: SFTY connects Travel Rule providers’ IVMS101 data with customer, wallet, routing, exceptions, case management and audit evidence into one operational compliance layer for exchanges.

problem_title: Travel Rule data remains disconnected across providers
problem_description: Exchanges face operational friction when originator and beneficiary data are fragmented between multiple Travel Rule providers. This separation causes delays in exception handling, weak linkage to compliance cases, and gaps in audit evidence for regulatory reviews.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates originator and beneficiary data from multiple Travel Rule providers into unified cases, linking message routing, exceptions, wallet activity, and audit evidence within one streamlined compliance workflow.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY moves Travel Rule messages through provider integration, routing, exception handling and case creation to preserve audit evidence for ongoing compliance review.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond message routing. Travel Rule evidence control

why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for Travel Rule operations?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects Travel Rule messages, customer data, routing status and exceptions into one structured compliance workflow for exchanges and digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle Travel Rule exceptions?
faq_2_answer: SFTY tracks and routes exception cases for incomplete or high-risk Travel Rule events, supporting review, decision logging and evidence preservation within a connected workflow.
faq_3_question: How is Travel Rule audit evidence preserved?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and supporting evidence for Travel Rule reviews to support audit-ready records aligned with regulatory and internal compliance requirements.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate existing Travel Rule providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY works as a compliance layer that connects multiple Travel Rule providers’ outputs into integrated workflows without disrupting existing provider operations.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace Travel Rule compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace Travel Rule providers but structures their outputs into actionable cases, policies, rules updates and reporting workflows workflows.
final_title: Connect Travel Rule data into structured cases
final_description: Review how SFTY integrates IVMS101 provider outputs with routing, exceptions, customer data and audit-ready evidence workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
